body:
Current situation:

value: any
- Select
- Menu Radio
- Menu Radio Group
- Toggle Group
- Tabs

value: unknown
- Field (in `validate` callback)
- Radio
- Radio Group

value: string
- Toggle Group
- Select.Value (in `children` render function)

***

My preferences/suggestions:
- We can't use `unknown` because it comes with a difficult DX
- `any` is OK but might be a footgun with non-primitive values causing unexpected rerenders due to different references. I could go either way though as it's more powerful too.
  - Any potential issues with non-primitive values in forms?
- Toggle needs to align with whatever everything else is using
- Select.Value needs to use the actual `value` type for the `children` render function

**Search keywords**:

comments.body: I'm a consumer of Base UI here. I've discovered that many of the `value` props cannot be typed. It would be ideal if some props could take a generic `T` to specify the type.
@cgatian Thanks for using Base UI ~ could you share a bit more about use-cases where this is an issue for you and which components?
This is a problem when implementing components, such as `RadioGroup`, especially when using `onValueChange`, which ends up with a following TypeScript error, and cannot be side-stepped.
For RadioGroup we could change it from unknown to string to match Checkbox(Group)
I was hoping you would do the other way around @mj12albert, i.e. support non-string values in Checkbox(Group). 🫠
The type for `RadioGroup` (until there are generics for all `value`s) should at least be `any`, which matches `Menu.RadioGroup`, not `unknown`.

labels.name: breaking change
labels.description: Introduces changes that are not backward compatible.

labels.name: scope: all components
labels.description: Widespread work has an impact on almost all components.

title: Decide on a common generic `value` type
